_source: Text from https://wii.hacks.guide — kept locally so errors show in-app.

items.error: Connection test fails with error 521xx
items.fix: Double-check the DNS entry: Primary DNS must be `3.143.163.250`. Make sure `Auto-Obtain DNS` is set to `No` and you entered this on the right page (the one with 2 fields, not 3).

items.error: Normal User Agreements show up instead of the exploit page
items.fix: Your ISP or network environment blocks custom DNS servers. Switch to a different network, or use a different exploit like **LetterBomb** (requires an SD card).

items.error: Hanging. or ERROR! if_config (ret = ...)
items.fix: The exploit failed to load. Restart your Wii and try again. If it persists, your network may be blocking the server — try using a mobile hotspot or another connection.

items.error: The HackMii Installer shows a black screen after the exploit
items.fix: Restart the Wii and try again. Make sure the date on your Wii is set correctly. If you recently installed CTGP Revolution or Project+, remove your SD card before running str2hax.

items.error: Press 1 doesn't appear on the scam warning screen
items.fix: Wait a full 30 seconds. The `Press 1 to continue` text is delayed. If it still doesn't appear, restart the Wii and trigger the exploit again.

items.error: Homebrew Channel install fails or freezes
items.fix: Restart the Wii and re-trigger the exploit. If it persists, reformat the SD card as FAT32 with MBR partitioning and try again.

items.error: Black screen and blue disc drive light blinking when launching BootMii
items.fix: BootMii is missing its SD card files. Download the BootMii SD files zip from `https://wii.hacks.guide/assets/files/bootmii_sd_files.zip`, extract it to the root of your SD card, and try again.

items.error: Launch BootMii button doesn't appear in Homebrew Channel
items.fix: BootMii wasn't installed successfully. Re-launch the HackMii Installer (re-trigger the exploit) and install BootMii again.

items.error: Exception IOS occurred! when launching nanddumper@ios
items.fix: Simply restart your Wii and try again.

items.error: No storage devices are attached (in nanddumper@ios)
items.fix: Make sure your SD card or USB drive is directly connected to the Wii. USB drives should not go through a hub.

items.error: Bad blocks during NAND backup verification
items.fix: Bad blocks are normal and mostly originate from the factory due to NAND binning. If all non-bad blocks are green after verification, the backup is fine.

items.error: Priiloader can't find hacks_hash.ini
items.fix: If you copied the installer to a USB drive, make sure you DON'T have an SD card inserted at the same time. The presence of both confuses Priiloader.

items.error: Priiloader doesn't load when holding RESET
items.fix: Make sure you're holding RESET (the red button near the eject button) while pressing POWER. On Wii Family Edition, hold RESET while the console is already on from the Wii Menu.

items.error: Wii doesn't recognize the SD card
items.fix: The SD card must be formatted as **FAT32** (not exFAT or NTFS) with **MBR** partitioning. Cards larger than 32GB may need a third-party tool to format as FAT32. Also check that the physical lock switch on the SD card is in the **unlocked** position (up).

items.error: SD card not showing in Data Management
items.fix: Turn the Wii off, remove and reinsert the card, and try again. If it still doesn't show, reformat the card as FAT32.

items.error: Exception 0700 occurred!
items.fix: This is usually a failed connection attempt. Power-cycle the console and retry — make sure no Wii Remotes are synced or nearby when you start.

items.error: Sync button presses don't register / the Pico's LED never flashes rapidly
items.fix: Keep the console within about 3 feet of the microcontroller, and double check the firmware you flashed matches your console's exact region and System Menu version.

items.error: The normal Wii Menu boots instead of the HackMii Installer
items.fix: The firmware file is for the wrong region or System Menu version. Re-check the version letter in Wii Settings and re-flash the correct .uf2 file.

items.error: The Internet Channel freezes or the cursor stops responding
items.fix: Restart the Wii and retry from the Internet Channel.

items.error: FlashHax fails on the first few attempts
items.fix: This is normal — FlashHax is unreliable and often needs several tries. Clear the Internet Channel's cookies and saved data if it keeps failing.

items.error: Still doesn't work after many attempts
items.fix: Switch to a different exploit — str2hax, LetterBomb, or Wilbrand are all more reliable if any of them apply to your console.

items.error: The bomb letter doesn't show up on the Message Board
items.fix: Check that the Wii's date is set correctly — try setting it a day earlier or later and check again.

items.error: please.hackmii.com rejects the MAC address
items.fix: Make sure you entered your own console's MAC address exactly, not an example from a screenshot or guide.

items.error: The console freezes on a black or blue screen after opening the letter
items.fix: You likely selected the wrong region. Power-cycle, confirm the region letter from Wii Settings, and regenerate the LetterBomb file.

items.error: SD card errors when copying the files
items.fix: Check Data Management on the Wii for format issues — the card must be FAT32.

items.error: The bomb letter doesn't show up on the Message Board
items.fix: Check that the Wii's date is set correctly, and confirm the SD card's physical lock switch is unlocked.

items.error: wilbrand.donut.eu.org rejects the MAC address
items.fix: Make sure you entered your own console's MAC address exactly, not an example from a screenshot or guide.

items.error: The console freezes on a black or blue screen after opening the letter
items.fix: Double-check the region, System Menu version, and MAC address you entered, then regenerate the file.

items.error: SD card larger than 2GB isn't recognized
items.fix: System Menu versions before 4.0 only support SD cards 2GB or smaller — use a smaller card or update first.

items.error: d2x cIOS Installer fails or hangs mid-install
items.fix: Make sure the Homebrew Channel is up to date and your SD/USB is FAT32 with MBR partitioning. Restart the Wii and retry — a partial install can safely be redone.

items.error: TMD version mismatch (Wii mini)
items.fix: Pick the base IOS revision matching your console's region (NTSC v31775 / PAL v31776) in the d2xl installer instead of the default selection.

items.error: Loader still says cIOS missing after install
items.fix: Confirm you installed all four slots (248-251) on a standard Wii, or cIOS 249 on Wii mini — installing only some of them isn't enough for every loader.

items.error: Console acts unstable after installing cIOS
items.fix: Never install a standard Wii/vWii cIOS build on a Wii mini or vice versa — reinstall using the correct installer for your exact model.

items.error: Forecast Channel shows FORE000006
items.fix: Your Wii's internal clock has drifted. Run the sntp homebrew app to resync it, then retry the channel.

items.error: A WiiLink channel won't connect at all
items.fix: Re-run the WiiLink Patcher from wiilink.ca/guide — channel server details change over time and old WADs can go stale.

items.error: vWii (Wii U) WiiConnect24 channels fail after working before
items.fix: Check that all three required Priiloader hacks for vWii are enabled, and note some hacks conflict — only one of the conflicting pair can be on at once.

items.error: Error 20100 or 20110
items.fix: The game you're trying to play isn't supported by Wiimmfi. Check the current game list at wiimmfi.de.

items.error: Error 23400 after setting custom DNS
items.fix: Your ISP or router is blocking custom DNS servers. Try a mobile hotspot, or set the DNS directly on your router instead of the console.

items.error: Priiloader Wiimmfi patch doesn't show v4
items.fix: Your hacks_hash.ini is outdated — update Priiloader's hack list before enabling the patch, or patching will fail with error 23904.

items.error: Patched ISO still won't connect online
items.fix: Confirm the file format is supported (ISO, WDF, WIA, CISO, WBFS, GCZ) — NKIT files can't be patched and need converting first.

items.error: CleanRip throws an exception during the redump.org download
items.fix: This is usually a network hiccup — restart CleanRip and try again.

items.error: Dump fails partway through
items.fix: Check your storage device has enough free space (4.7GB, or 8.5GB for dual-layer discs) and try a different USB port or SD card if it keeps failing.

items.error: Trying to dump a GameCube disc but the option isn't there
items.fix: Wii Family Edition, Wii mini, and vWii can't dump GameCube discs — only original Wii models can.

items.error: "Waiting for HDD..." for 20+ seconds
items.fix: Make sure d2x cIOS is installed (see the cios guide), and that your USB drive is FAT32 with MBR partitioning and 32kb allocation unit size.

items.error: No games showing up in the list
items.fix: Check your games are in the wbfs or games folder the loader expects, and in WiiFlow, make sure Startup Settings is searching the right device (SD vs USB).

items.error: A specific game crashes or hangs on launch
items.fix: Try a different cIOS slot (248-251) for that game — Settings > Game Load > Game IOS in USB Loader GX, or the gear icon > cIOS in WiiFlow.

items.error: csm-installer can't download the base theme
items.fix: Make sure the Wii is connected to the internet and retry — this step just pulls your console's own unmodified theme.

items.error: Theme looks corrupted or menu glitches after install
items.fix: The theme's version/region didn't match your console. Boot into Priiloader (hold RESET) and reinstall the correct base/custom theme pair.

items.error: Wii Menu won't boot after a theme install
items.fix: Use Priiloader to get back to a working System Menu, or restore your NAND backup with BootMii as a last resort.

items.error: SysCheck won't launch from Homebrew Channel
items.fix: Reinstall SysCheckME to your SD/USB root's apps folder — a partial extract is the usual cause.

items.error: Upload to syscheck.rc24.xyz fails
items.fix: Check your Wii's internet connection; the CSV is still saved locally so you can share it manually instead.

items.error: A WAD fails to install
items.fix: Confirm the WAD isn't corrupted (re-download it) and that it matches your console's region/type — mismatched WADs are the most common cause.

items.error: YAWM doesn't see my WAD files
items.fix: Put them in a folder named wad at the root of your SD card or USB drive — YAWM looks there automatically.

items.error: LibreShop won't connect to the store
items.fix: Check your Wii's internet connection. If it persists, fall back to Homebrew Browser or use TinyWiiBackupManager/OSCDL on a computer instead.

items.error: Downloaded app doesn't show up in Homebrew Channel
items.fix: Make sure it installed to the apps folder on the storage device you're actually booting the Homebrew Channel from.

items.error: Error:003 unauthorized device has been detected.
items.fix: This happens on region-changed Korean Wiis after a system update, from an encryption key mismatch. Recovery usually requires Recovery Mode access and a drivechip.

items.error: Console won't boot at all after the error appears
items.fix: Try Recovery Mode (see that guide) to get a disc-read screen and load a recovery exploit from there.

items.error: Installed the wrong region's System Menu
items.fix: This can semibrick the console (Opera browser errors in Wii Settings). Reinstall the correct regional System Menu/theme via YAWM ModMii Edition as soon as possible.

items.error: NUSGet download fails or times out
items.fix: Nintendo's NUS servers are occasionally slow or briefly unreachable — wait and retry. Double-check you unchecked the encryption option before downloading.

items.error: YAWM ModMii Edition doesn't see the WAD files
items.fix: Confirm all three WADs are directly inside a folder named exactly wad at the root of your SD/USB device, not in a subfolder.

items.error: Priiloader disappears after the update
items.fix: Make sure you selected 'yes' when asked whether to keep Priiloader during the install step — if it's gone, reinstall it via the Homebrew Channel.

items.error: nandBinCheck reports errors on the blank NAND
items.fix: Do not flash it. Ask for help in the Nintendo Homebrew Discord before proceeding — flashing a bad image can cause an unrecoverable brick.

items.error: Windows SmartScreen blocks Ohneschwanzenegger
items.fix: This is a known false positive from bundling homebrew tools. Click 'More info' then 'Run anyway' if you trust the source you downloaded it from.

items.error: Console won't boot after flashing the new NAND
items.fix: Double check you didn't overwrite your real backup, and that keys.bin on the SD card matches this exact console. If it still fails, you'll need your original NAND backup to try again.
